user_query:
I want a personalized setup for an autonomous betting agent that finds cross-market arbitrage between sportsbooks and prediction markets, on a tight monthly budget, and I’m comfortable with technical setup. Please read my answers in input/assessment.yaml and build the plan from that. Then:

1) Write a tailored checklist to output/stack.md using a four-layer agent framework. Include:
- A header "Your Agent Betting Stack"
- Goal, Budget, and an Estimated setup time
- Step 1 (Install Core Components), Step 2 (Configure APIs), Step 3 (Test Your Stack) with at least two concrete test prompts, Step 4 (Optional Upgrades) with clear reasons for each upgrade
- A "Relevant Guides" section with links
- A short "Risk & Notes" section clarifying that the recommended components are read-only and trade execution requires platform-specific integrations

2) Write a structured spec to output/stack.json with these fields:
- goal, budget, technical_level (from input/assessment.yaml)
- estimated_setup_time_minutes (integer)
- cost_tier ("Free", "Hobby", or "Pro")
- minimum_viable_stack (array of component names)
- recommended_skills (array of up to 7 items, highest priority first)
- layers_covered (object with keys L2, L3, L4; each an array of component names)
- guides (array of objects with name and url)

Rules:
- Organize everything by the four-layer framework and tailor to my goal/budget/skill from input/assessment.yaml
- Keep recommended_skills to 7 or fewer and prioritize them
- Include relevant guide links in both files
- In minimum_viable_stack, include the core cross-market scanning and arbitrage components for a hybrid approach (sportsbooks + prediction markets)
- Include at least one bankroll/wallet management element in layers_covered (L2)
- Do not include any trade execution steps; keep it read-only

Deliverables:
- output/stack.md and output/stack.json

Read input/assessment.yaml before you start.
